changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The functions map well to the PCI DSS 3.4.1 control, focusing on encryption and logging. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but the audit logging function needed refinement for clarity and consistency with other API server-related checks.
naming_improvements: Ensure all audit-related functions specify 'apiserver' to avoid ambiguity
title: Requirement 3.4.1
step1_reasoning: The PCI DSS 3.4.1 control focuses on encryption and logging, which are directly applicable to Kubernetes through the encryption of secrets and etcd, as well as enabling audit logging. Kubernetes provides mechanisms to encrypt secrets at rest and etcd, ensuring sensitive data is protected. Additionally, Kubernetes audit logging can be configured to ensure that all access and changes are logged, supporting compliance with audit requirements.
